statement: Stage activities are too coarse for full-scale pilot execution, and the current five-minute start-to-close timeout should either be raised or the work should be partitioned so one activity does not serially process an entire stage worth of provider calls.
rationale: Whole-stage sequential loops under a fixed five-minute activity timeout are likely to become brittle under full V3 scale and provider latency.
keyAssumptions: Future pilot runs will process enough units for stage duration to be materially larger than small smoke tests.
validationCriteria: Pilot-scale stages no longer risk timing out under normal latency.
Timeout behavior is explicit and testable.
critiquePoints: Observed smoke success may mean current scale is still within safe bounds for now.
questions: Do you want finer activity granularity, larger timeouts, or both?
